flags.concern: timeline-video-duration: the plan routes dropped videos through a new generation path but does not preserve duration metadata in the asset registry. In this repo, inserted video clips fall back to 5 seconds when `registry.duration` is missing, and the existing multi-generation video path explicitly patches duration from drag metadata before dropping. Following the plan as written would still create generation-backed videos that are timed incorrectly on the timeline.
flags.evidence: `buildAssetDropEdit()` uses `assetEntry.duration ?? 5` for video/media clips in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:148-155` and `:185-206`. The current generation-video drop path patches `duration` via `getDroppedGenerationDuration()` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/external-drop-utils.ts:46-55` and `:394-400`, and the existing test asserts that patch in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx:300-331`.

flags.concern: asset-panel-upload-routing: step 3 names `AssetPanel.tsx`, but the actual file-upload behavior is behind the `onUploadFiles` callback and `useAssetOperations.uploadFiles()`, which currently sends every file straight through `uploadAsset()`. Updating `AssetPanel` alone will not create generation-backed registry entries for uploaded videos or attach `generationId`; the plan needs to touch the callback plumbing that sits behind the panel upload button.
flags.evidence: `AssetPanel` only forwards the selected files to `onUploadFiles(files)` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/AssetPanel.tsx:172-184`. The commented-out panel wiring passes `uploadFiles` directly in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/PropertiesPanel.tsx:106-128`, and `uploadFiles` currently does `Promise.all(files.map(uploadAsset))` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetOperations.ts:41-47`.

flags.concern: video-thumbnail-helper-surface: the implementation step calls for importing `extractThumbnailFromVideo` from `videoThumbnailGenerator.ts`, but that helper is private in this repository. If executed literally, that step will not compile; the public surface today is `extractAndUploadThumbnailOnly()` or the file-based `extractVideoPosterFrame()` helper.
flags.evidence: `extractThumbnailFromVideo` is a non-exported local function in `src/shared/lib/media/videoThumbnailGenerator.ts:39-57`. The file instead exports `extractAndUploadThumbnailOnly` at `src/shared/lib/media/videoThumbnailGenerator.ts:148-168`, and the repo also exports `extractVideoPosterFrame` from `src/shared/lib/media/videoPosterExtractor.ts:3-9`.
